---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Lester"
date: "2023-03-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(UpSetR)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
```

## Load data from excel
```{r cars}
files <- list.files("C:/Users/Leste/Karchin Lab Dropbox/YunZhou Liu/PanIN-2023/PICTograph Mutation Calls 1.31.23/", full.names = T)
file_list <- lapply(files, function(x) read_excel(x)%>%
                      select(contains(c("Hugo_Symbol","HGVSp_Short")))%>%
                      mutate(file = gsub(".*31.23/(.*) Filter.*","\\1",x)))


## excels has duplicated columns, and for AB_14_1.3&1.4 two columns of HGVSp is not the same

ff <- lapply(1:length(file_list), function(x) file_list[[x]][!duplicated(as.list(file_list[[x]]))]%>%
                      select(c(1:2,"file"))%>%
                      rename("Hugo_Symbol" = grep("Hugo",colnames(.)),
                             "HGVSp_Short" = grep("HGVS", colnames(.))))%>%
  do.call(rbind,.)%>%
  mutate(gene = paste0(Hugo_Symbol,HGVSp_Short),
         reigon = gsub("(.*)\\..*","\\1",file),
         reigon = gsub(" ","_",reigon))%>%
  group_by(file)%>%
  distinct(gene, .keep_all = T)%>%
  ungroup()%>%
  select(gene, file, reigon)

reigon <- unique(ff$reigon)
# remove TC98_2 bc 98_2.5 has duplicated gene name
#reigon <- reigon[-12]
new_rei <- c("TC_92","AB_14","AB_17")

# this function still consider each panin reigono seperately
get_list <- function(rei, dat){
  
  mut<- dat%>%
    filter(grepl(rei,.$reigon))%>%
    mutate(type = gene)%>%
    spread(key = file, value = gene)
  
  order = colnames(mut[,-c(1,2)])
  
  temp <- mut[,-c(1,2)]%>%
    mutate_all(function(x) ifelse(is.na(x),0,1))%>%
    unite("presence", colnames(.),sep = "", remove = F)
  summ <- lapply(unique(temp$presence), function(x) mut$type[temp$presence==x])
  names(summ) = unique(temp$presence)
  
  mut_ <- as.list(mut[,-c(1,2)])
  
  for (i in 1:length(mut_)){
    mut_[[i]] <- mut_[[i]][!is.na(mut_[[i]])]
  }
  
     
  out <- list(mut = mut_, presence = summ, order = order)
  return(out)
}



dat_in <- lapply(reigon, function(x) get_list(x,ff))
```



## load from rds
```{r}
files <- list.files("C:/Users/Leste/Karchin Lab Dropbox/YunZhou Liu/for-lester (1)/pictograph-input-PanIN-AB", full.names = T)

reigon <- list.files("C:/Users/Leste/Karchin Lab Dropbox/YunZhou Liu/for-lester (1)/pictograph-input-PanIN-AB")

file_list <- lapply(files, function(x) list(y = readRDS(x)[[2]],
                                            mut_id = readRDS(x)[[1]]))
get_list <- function(a_list){
  mut <- a_list[[2]]
  y <- a_list[[1]]%>%
    as.data.frame()%>%
    mutate_all(function(x) ifelse(x == 0,NA,mut))%>%
    as.list()
  summary <- a_list[[1]]%>%
    as.data.frame()%>%
    mutate_all(function(x) ifelse(x != 0,"1","0"))%>%
    unite("type", colnames(.),sep = "", remove = F)
  summ <- lapply(unique(summary$type), function(x) as.data.frame(mut[summary$type==x]))
  names(summ) = unique(summary$type)
  for (i in 1:length(y)){
    y[[i]] <- y[[i]][!is.na(y[[i]])]
  }
  
  out <- list(y = y, mut = summ)
  return(out)
}

dat_in <- lapply(file_list, function(x) get_list(x))
names(dat_in) <- gsub("panin_number_(.*)_picto.*","\\1",reigon)
```

# UPSETr
```{r}
upset(fromList(dat_in[[6]][[1]]), text.scale = 1.7, nsets = 100, 
      keep.order = T, sets = c(dat_in[[6]][[3]]))
```
